county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Original 1850 county. Named for Chief Marin of the Coast Miwok. San Rafael Mission (1817) records are among the earliest. Marin County Free Library has local history collection. Point Reyes was site of Francis Drake's 1579 landing.
county_metadata.research_tips: Marin County Recorder in San Rafael has deeds from 1850. Mission San Rafael Arcangel (1817) records are among the earliest in the county — check Archdiocese of San Francisco. Marin County Free Library's Anne T. Kent California Room has extensive local history holdings including the Marin County Journal newspaper files. Mexican-era ranchos (Rancho Nicasio, Rancho Corte Madera del Presidio) provide land records for pre-statehood families. Coast Miwok tribal records through NARA. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. Point Reyes Station historical records trace dairy farming families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, San Rafael — index and images vary by era

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptismal, marriage, and burial records from Mission San Rafael Arcangel (1817). Spanish colonial era records documenting Native American converts, settlers, and soldiers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mission records generally well preserved; some at Huntington Library or Santa Barbara Mission Archive-Library

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Original 1850 county, once the largest in California, stretching to Nevada and Utah borders. Mother Lode Gold Rush county. Yosemite Valley is within Mariposa County. John C. Fremont's Mariposa Estate grant is a key land record.
county_metadata.research_tips: Mariposa County Recorder has records from 1850. Originally the largest county in California, extending to the Nevada border. Mariposa Museum and History Center preserves Gold Rush records. John C. Fremont's Mariposa Estate land grant case reached the US Supreme Court — court files are extensive. Yosemite Valley records relate to early tourism and the Ahwahneechee people. Mining claim records at BLM are critical. The Mariposa Gazette newspaper (from 1854) provides early family records. Many counties were carved from Mariposa, so researchers must track boundary changes carefully.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, Mariposa — index and images vary by era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Original 1850 county. Named for Cape Mendocino. Lumber industry and coastal communities. Round Valley Reservation (1856) was one of California's earliest reservations. Significant Italian and Portuguese settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Mendocino County Recorder in Ukiah has deeds from 1850. Round Valley Reservation (established 1856) records are held by tribal government and NARA. Mendocino County Museum in Willits has logging and ranching community records. Italian immigrants in the wine and fishing industries are well documented. Portuguese settlers worked in the dairy and lumber industries. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The Mendocino Beacon and Ukiah Dispatch-Democrat newspapers provide early obituaries. Coast Miwok and Pomo tribal records through NARA BIA files. Timber industry records trace lumber families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, Ukiah — index and images vary by era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1855 from Mariposa County. Named for the Merced River. Central Valley agricultural county. Castle Air Force Base (1941-1995) records significant for military families. Growing population from Dust Bowl migration.
county_metadata.research_tips: Merced County Recorder has deeds from 1855. Pre-1855 records in Mariposa County. Merced County Courthouse Museum has local history holdings. Castle Air Force Base (1941-1995) records at NARA trace military families stationed in the area. Central Valley agricultural records supplement census data. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. Check the Merced Sun-Star newspaper for obituaries. Significant Portuguese dairy farming community. Yokuts tribal homeland — check NARA BIA records. Merced River irrigation districts generated records useful for tracing farming families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, Merced — index and images vary by era

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Annual property tax assessments from 1855. Valuable for tracking land ownership, crop types, and agricultural improvements in the San Joaquin/Sacramento Valley.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1874 from Siskiyou County. Named for the Modoc people. Site of the Modoc War (1872-1873), the only major Indian war in California. Alturas is county seat. Remote northeastern corner of California. Ranching economy.
county_metadata.research_tips: Modoc County Recorder in Alturas has deeds from 1874. Pre-1874 records in Siskiyou County. The Modoc War (1872-1873) generated extensive military and government records at NARA — Captain Jack's Stronghold is a National Monument. Modoc County Museum preserves ranching heritage records. This remote northeastern California county has a small population, making family research more manageable. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Check the Modoc County Record newspaper for obituaries and legal notices. BLM land patents trace homesteaders in this high desert ranching county.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, Alturas — index and images vary by era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1861 from Calaveras and Fresno counties. Eastern Sierra Nevada county. Bodie ghost town was a major mining camp. Mono Lake is ecologically significant. Small population, primarily mining and ranching history.
county_metadata.research_tips: Mono County Recorder in Bridgeport has deeds from 1861. Bodie State Historic Park archives preserve records of this famous ghost town, once a boomtown of 10,000. Pre-1861 records split between Calaveras and Fresno counties. Eastern Sierra Museum in Independence covers Mono County history. Mining claim records at BLM are critical for tracing Bodie, Aurora, and Mammoth mining families. Mono Lake Committee has environmental history records. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Small population means Great Register voter rolls and county assessment records are particularly useful.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, Bridgeport — index and images vary by era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Original 1850 county. Monterey was California's capital under Spain and Mexico. Presidio and mission records date to 1770. Colton Hall, site of California's 1849 Constitutional Convention. Salinas Valley agriculture, Steinbeck country.
county_metadata.research_tips: Monterey County Recorder in Salinas has records from 1850. Monterey was the capital of California under Spain and Mexico — Presidio records date to 1770 and mission records to 1770. Colton Hall, where the 1849 California Constitution was written, is a museum. The Monterey County Historical Society has extensive holdings. John Steinbeck's Cannery Row documents the sardine industry community. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records extensively. Archdiocese of Monterey holds Catholic church records. Check the Salinas Californian newspaper for obituaries. Mexican-era land grant records are essential.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, Salinas — index and images vary by era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Original 1850 county. Wappo and Patwin tribal homeland. Wine industry dates to 1838. Napa State Hospital (1875) records significant. Early settlers included George Yount (1831) and other Mexican-era land grant holders.
county_metadata.research_tips: Napa County Recorder has deeds from 1850. Napa County Historical Society holds local records and photographs. George Yount's 1831 settlement predates American occupation. Mexican-era land grants (Rancho Carne Humana, Rancho Caymus) provide early property records. Wine industry records from the 1830s trace pioneer families. Napa State Hospital (1875) patient records may be accessible through California State Archives. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The Napa Valley Register newspaper provides obituaries from the 1860s. Wappo and Patwin tribal records through NARA.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, Napa — index and images vary by era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1851 from Yuba County. Named for the Sierra Nevada. Grass Valley and Nevada City were major Gold Rush towns. Rich hard-rock mining (Empire Mine operated 1850-1956). Extensive mining claim records.
county_metadata.research_tips: Nevada County Recorder in Nevada City has deeds from 1851. The Searls Historical Library in Nevada City has outstanding Gold Rush-era manuscripts and local history records. Grass Valley and Nevada City were major hard-rock mining centers — the Empire Mine operated from 1850 to 1956, generating extensive employee and production records. Mining claim records at BLM are essential. Cornish miners ('Cousin Jacks') and Chinese laborers well documented. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The Nevada City Herald and Grass Valley Telegraph newspapers provide early obituaries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: County Recorder, Nevada City — index and images vary by era

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1889 from Los Angeles County. Named for its citrus groves. Rapid 20th-century suburbanization. Japanese farming communities before WWII. Disneyland opened 1955. Richard Nixon Presidential Library in Yorba Linda.
county_metadata.research_tips: Orange County Recorder in Santa Ana has deeds from 1889. Pre-1889 records in Los Angeles County. Orange County Archives has local history holdings. Japanese farming communities in Huntington Beach and Westminster thrived before WWII internment. Citrus industry heritage records trace early growers. The First American Corporation title plant has indexed county deed records. Richard Nixon Presidential Library in Yorba Linda has regional history. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. Check the Santa Ana Register newspaper for obituaries. Irvine Ranch Company records document a major land grant family.
